flaws.summary: PELT shared covariance pre-estimated with global mean-centering instead of pooled within-segment covariance. Global-centering conflates between-segment variation with within-segment variation when change points exist, inflating covariance estimates and biasing NLL computation.

Overflow redistribution loop breaks without resolution when all 4 phases hit g_max. Bare break leaves total_g > G_avail, triggering assertion failure at runtime. Latent bug avoided in current run by parameter coincidence.

Parallel trend test uses averaged t-statistic compared to t-distribution instead of proper joint F-test for H0: all pre-treatment coefficients = 0. Average of t-statistics follows neither t nor F distribution.
Synthetic data provides exact ground truth (120 injected cruising plates) but code never computes precision/recall/F1. Detected count 210 vs ground truth 120 -- model accuracy unknown.
PELT pruning is functionally a no-op. No candidate pruned by optimality condition. Algorithm runs O(n^2) rather than advertised O(n).
Speed proxy hardcodes 500m between records. Actual segment lengths 700m (intersections 1-8) and 360m (intersections 9-12). For shorter segments, speeds overestimated by ~39%.
Tau sensitivity CV = 56.2%. Parking demand varies 7x (15 to 102 spaces) across tau=[15,60]. Output depends more on tuning parameter than on data.
Cruise counts and feature samples for fig6 from separate re-run of estimate_parking_demand(), not from sensitivity_analysis() internal results.
Offset mutation uses child[0] (C_0) as ub, but SBX crosses C_0 and offsets independently. Post-mutation repair corrects but GA explores unnecessarily large space.
GA converges exactly to heuristic initialization offsets (63.0s / 32.4s = travel time at 11.11 m/s). Not validated against random initialization. Elite dominance not ruled out.
Variable holidays = {5,6,7,8,9,10} declared but never used. Actual holiday detection uses different arithmetic.
Cohen d sign flip for flow/wait metrics is undocumented domain knowledge. Reduction = improvement should be explicit.
All R-squared values below 0.2 (avg_speed: 0.066, total_flow: 0.198, waiting_time: 0.067). Post coefficient beta_3 not significant for any metric.
Breakpoints nearly identical across date types (weekday=weekend=[28,36,56,68,76,84], holiday differs by 1 position). Correct for synthetic scaled data but needs explanation.
GA runs exactly 200 generations without any convergence check or early stopping.
Cruising time series subplot conditionally empty if cruise_counts is None. Empty subplot in 2x2 grid looks like an error.
Single GA run trace without variance information. Standard practice: multiple runs with mean/std band.
Green wave diagram shows only Phase 1 (N-S straight) bands. Other three phases invisible without annotation.
Feature scatter (8000 vehicles) small markers alpha=0.6 no edge color. Dense clusters obscure distribution. Hexbin recommended.
Title says lower-left quadrant for cruising, but cruising requires H >= H_threshold (above threshold line). Confusing spatial description.
Coefficient labels auto-abbreviated (avg_s_Post, tot_Post) may be illegible at print resolution.
Colorbar shrink=0.8 across 3 subplots; movement labels fontsize=8 may be hard to read in print.
Phase-1 DID circular reasoning fix VERIFIED. All 12 intersections treated; no outcome-based division.
Phase-1 GA residual bug fix VERIFIED. Three-step g_min->remainder->overflow design with assertions.
Phase-1 PELT BIC over-penalty fix VERIFIED. Penalty = 0.5 * ln(96) * 12.
Phase-1 segment_length undefined fix VERIFIED. AVG_SEGMENT_LENGTH=530.0 defined.
Phase-1 critical phase delay proxy VERIFIED. Model design section 2.5 provides justification.

t_spread = min(t_A, t_B) takes the smaller of two sources' spread completion times, but the spread phase is not complete until the later source finishes. Should be max(t_A, t_B). Affects all downstream t_spread-dependent quantities.

Uses (t_A + t_B)/2 as base spread time for edge_0_3m_time/edge_full_time computation. Arithmetic mean of two independent spread times has no physical meaning. Combined volume evolution is Q*t + Q*max(0, t-t_offset_B). The averaging introduces systematic bias in all flood-time-dependent computations for Problem 3 and Problem 4.

plot_dual_source_collision receives empty WaterState for source B. Figure 7 will only show A's single-source coverage and blocked edges, without any B-source coverage visualization. This is critically misleading for a competition paper.

3D model T_total=626.63 min only accounts for filling all levels to H0=0.1m, not H_MAX=3m. The summary report labels this as 'T_total' without clarifying it is only the 0.1m fill time. Full 3m fill would be ~30x longer. edge_0_3m_time and edge_full_time for upper-level edges remain unfilled.

t_meet = (V_total + Q_B*t_offset_B) / Q_total adds Q_B*t_offset_B volume term treating B's offset flow as accumulated in this specific tunnel. B's water spreads through its own network during offset, not in this tunnel. Also uses instantaneous edge-level flow rates Q_A/Q_B for cumulative volume estimation, which is dynamically incorrect.

Single source T_total = max(edge_full_time) (proportional allocation per edge length). Dual source T_total = total_volume/(2*Q) (pure conservation). Two methods produce inconsistent results: 12420.57 vs expected 6210.29, actual 6389.05 (2.9% deviation). Parallel reporting would confuse readers.

3D parallel upward spread fills each level to H0 sequentially but does not account for raising lower levels from H0 to H_MAX while upper levels fill. Model treats levels as sequential and independent, but real physics is parallel and dependent. Underestimates total time and misorders level arrival times.

Problem 2 escape time (1.42 min) < Problem 4 T_SWITCH (5.0 min). Miner reaches exit before replanning trigger. replan_path degenerates to restarting from start node. No actual replanning occurs with synthetic data. Must be documented.
Module docstring claims '.total_time attribute removed from PathResult' but line 150 self.total_time = total_time still stores it. Not a runtime bug but misleading documentation.

56 blocked edges (~37% of ~150 edges in synthetic 80-node network) reported from dual-source collision without any real-data comparison baseline. Modeling phase validation reference values unverified.
Returned dual-source WaterState has empty edge_spread_time dict. Although downstream code uses edge_0_3m_time instead (which is populated), the WaterState data structure is incomplete.
escape_time unit column shows '-' instead of 'min', inconsistent with other rows like '总灌满时间(双源)' which correctly shows 'min'.

errors.description: Algorithm 8 (Q5 simplified) line 3 references 'theta_j0' (UAV initial heading angle) to compute dir_UAV_j for the Hungarian cost matrix. This variable is not in the function signature SolveProblem5(U_j0[5][3], M_m0[3][3], params, N_max=3) and is not defined anywhere else. The cost matrix computation is blocked. The modeling hand's self-check claims '已避免ERR-A-018（未定义变量）' but theta_j0 is precisely such an undefined variable.
errors.directive: Add theta_j0[5] as an explicit input parameter to SolveProblem5, or specify that it is part of params, or derive it from U_j0 positions with an explicit formula.
errors.correction: theta_j0[5] added to SolveProblem5 function signature as explicit input parameter; theta_{j0} added to symbol table.

errors.description: Algorithm 8 lines 15-21 assign spare UAVs only to EMPTY groups. But the 5x5 Hungarian algorithm (line 9) assigns exactly one UAV per column. Columns 0,1,2 (real missiles) each receive exactly one UAV. Therefore groups[m] is NEVER empty for m=0,1,2. The fallback never fires. The 2 spare UAVs are permanently wasted. The mathematical model explicitly describes |J_m| >= 2 cases using SolveProblem4 (Q4), but this code path is structurally unreachable.
errors.directive: Replace the 'empty group' fallback with a mechanism that distributes spare UAVs to augment existing groups (e.g., assign each spare UAV to the group with the shortest occlusion time), enabling |J_m| >= 2 code paths.
errors.correction: Replaced Hungarian algorithm with two-phase greedy assignment: Phase 1 ensures 1 UAV per missile (basic coverage), Phase 2 assigns remaining 2 UAVs by global best score (enabling |J_m|>=2).

errors.description: Algorithm 7 (SolveProblem4) line 4 unpacks 3 return values (x_opt_j, T_j, intervals_j) from SolveProblem2, but SolveProblem2 (Algorithm 5 line 35) returns only 2 values: (best_x_overall, T_overall_best). The intervals_j variable is never returned. This cascades to Algorithm 8 line 31 where SolveProblem4 is expected to return merged intervals as the third value. Pre-existing bug, but now affects the Q5 code path.
errors.directive: Modify SolveProblem2 to return intervals, or restructure the Q4 code path to recompute intervals from x_opt after optimization.
errors.correction: SolveProblem2 return signature changed to (x_opt, T_opt, best_interval) triple; all call sites (Algorithm 7 Stage A1, Algorithm 8 Level 2 via Q3/Q4) now consistently unpack 3 values.

errors.description: Algorithm 8 lines 33-38 flatten intervals using 'flat.extend(sub)' -- this expects each sub to be an iterable of intervals (list-of-lists format from SolveProblem3). If SolveProblem4 returns a flat merged interval list (as its name 'merged_intervals' suggests), then 'sub' would be a single (start,end) tuple and extend would corrupt flat into [s, e, s, e, ...]. This is a latent bug since ERR-A-032 makes the |J_m| >= 2 code path unreachable.
errors.directive: After fixing ERR-A-032, ensure the flattening logic handles both SolveProblem3 (list-of-lists) and SolveProblem4 (flat list) output formats correctly.
errors.correction: Unified SolveProblem3 and SolveProblem4 to both return flat interval lists [(s1,e1), (s2,e2), ...]. Algorithm 8 Level 2 interval merge and Level 3 scanline intersection now process consistent flat format.

errors.description: The problem_analysis section acknowledges '跨组空间重叠不再强制约束' as a known limitation, but this is not documented in the mathematical_model (Q5 Level 2) or algorithm_flow (Algorithm 8) sections. Without explicit documentation, the paper writer may not flag this as a limitation in the final paper.
errors.directive: Add an explicit note in the mathematical_model Q5 section and Algorithm 8 that cross-group spatial overlap is not constrained, and this is a known simplification.
errors.correction: Added explicit note in mathematical_model Q5 section (lines 401-404) and algorithm_flow boundary cases (item 11) about cross-group spatial overlap being unconstrained, with tactical justification and mitigation in sensitivity analysis.

errors.description: Q5 returns 0.00s intersection because ALL three missile groups produce empty occlusion intervals (Q5_M0/M1/M2_intervals.csv have zero data rows). Root cause: task assignment Algorithm 8 Layer 1 uses heading-angle-only score function without checking geometric feasibility -- whether any UAV position can produce a cloud intersecting the missile's line of sight. Example: UAV0 at (3000,0,500) assigned to Missile2 at (4000,-6928,150): minimum UAV-to-line-of-sight distance ~2600m >> R_s=50m. Code silently accepts empty intervals with no diagnostic.
errors.directive: Add geometric feasibility filter to task assignment: compute perpendicular distance from each UAV's reachable region to each missile's line of sight; exclude infeasible pairings before assignment. Report which pairings fail and why.
errors.correction: Per-group geometric feasibility diagnostic added in SolveProblem5 (lines 1610-1621 and 1673-1733). Q5_feasibility_diagnosis.csv now reports per-UAV perpendicular distance to missile LOS, is_geometrically_feasible flag, and geo_note text for each pairing. The diagnostic clearly identifies all 5 pairings as geometrically infeasible (perp_dist 2640-3041m >> R_s=50m). However, the diagnostic is post-hoc (after assignment) rather than a pre-filter. The underlying assignment criterion is unchanged. Verified: CSV contains 6 rows with full geometric analysis.

errors.description: Q2 optimization (SolveProblem2, pattern search + DE) converged to theta=3.122rad, v_u=45.63m/s, t_d=1.66s, t_b=10.51s with T_eff=8.13s (validation precision), which is 8.6% WORSE than the Q1 heuristic default (theta=pi, v_u=30, t_d=0, t_b=9, T_eff=8.90s) for the same scenario. Code does not compare optimization result against heuristic baseline or detect the regression.
errors.directive: After DE convergence, evaluate a heuristic baseline (e.g., theta=initial heading, v_u=30, t_d=0, t_b=9) at same precision and keep whichever is better. Log when optimization regresses.
errors.correction: evaluate_heuristic_q2 function added (lines 97-107). Stage-C fallback comparison added in SolveProblem2 (lines 887-898): evaluates Q1 heuristic baseline (theta=pi, v_u=30, t_d=0, t_b=9) and keeps whichever is better. When optimization regresses, prints warning and uses heuristic. Verified: Q2 result (theta=3.1416, v_u=30, t_d=0, t_b=9, T_eff=8.90s) matches heuristic. However, the fact that optimization found NOTHING better than the heuristic remains a concern -- the fallback masks the optimization failure rather than fixing it.

errors.description: Q3 with 3 bombs produces T_eff=8.19s -- 8% LESS than Q1 with 1 bomb (8.90s). Bombs 2 and 3 contribute zero occlusion because burst altitudes are too high (above the missile's line of sight). The retry threshold at line 1188 compares against the first bomb's own suboptimal result (~8.19s) with factor 0.9, so 8.19 > 8.19*0.9 = 7.37, never triggering. The threshold should compare against the GLOBAL best single-bomb baseline (8.90s from Q1 heuristic), not the first bomb's degraded result.
errors.directive: Replace retry threshold with comparison against the Q1 heuristic baseline (t_d=0, t_b=9) evaluated at validation precision. The 3-bomb result MUST be >= the single-bomb optimum, not just >= 0.9 * first-bomb-alone.
errors.correction: Single-bomb baseline comparison added (lines 1180-1247), but retry threshold `actual_total < T_single * 0.9` has two defects: (a) T_single is the FIRST bomb's own solitary interval (~8.19s), not the global Q1 baseline (8.90s); (b) the 0.9 factor allows up to 10% degradation instead of requiring improvement. Threshold must be fixed.

errors.description: DE optimization results are not reliably reproducible despite np.random.seed(42). Sequential calls to SolveProblem2 (Q2), SolveProblem3 (Q3), SolveProblem4 (Q4), and SolveProblem5 (Q5) all consume the same RNG state in sequence.
errors.location: solver.py > lines 31 (seed), 633-637 (random init), 761-762 (DE pop init), 1050 (Q3 RNG), 1457 (Q4 RNG), 2545 (Q5 seed)
errors.directive: Use independent RandomState instances for each subproblem.
errors.correction: Independent RNG states: Q3 uses q3_rng = np.random.RandomState(43) (line 1050), Q4 uses q4_rng = np.random.RandomState(44) (line 1457), Q5 uses np.random.seed(45) (line 2545). Q1 and Q2 both use np.random.seed(42) but Q1 consumes no randomness, so functional correctness holds. Verified: independent streams for Q3/Q4/Q5.

errors.description: Q5 task assignment score function (solver.py lines 1357-1367) uses INITIAL heading theta_j0 to compute UAV-to-missile suitability scores, but Q2/Q4 optimization can change the UAV's actual heading (theta is a decision variable). A UAV with poor initial heading alignment (high score = bad) might achieve perfect occlusion by turning, while a well-aligned initial heading does not guarantee the UAV will maintain that heading during optimization. The assignment score and the actual optimized trajectory are decoupled.
errors.directive: Either (a) use a geometric bound on the UAV's reachable region independent of heading, or (b) recompute the score after optimization in Layer 2 and flag mismatches.

errors.description: Q5 task assignment score function (heading alignment) is geometrically decoupled from occlusion feasibility. All 5 UAVs assigned with perpendicular distances 2600-3041m >> R_s=50m. Q5 returns 0.00s for default scenario. This is a model design limitation inherited by the code.

errors.description: Q5_feasibility_diagnosis.csv is stale data (file timestamp 15:41 vs 20:33 for other results). Contains only 5 of 15 UAV-missile pairs with perp distances 2640-3041 m, but omits the key pair (UAV 0 to M0 LOS, perp ~444 m) that the paper cites as the global minimum. The final run's feasibility pre-check caused early return before diagnostic code executed, leaving old CSV unmodified.

Q4 degradation explanation (Section 5.4: T_eff=7.43 s vs Q1 8.90 s) attributes loss to 'DE fine-tuning shifting parameters away from Q1 optimum in an unsuccessful attempt to generate cross-UAV synergy.' Causal claim about optimizer intent is unsupported: no Q4 decision variable values are compared against Q1/Q2 optimum, and no evidence shows DE attempted cross-UAV coupling.

Section 6.1 (R_s sensitivity) uses qualitative language only: 'widens this window approximately linearly,' 'would bring some marginal pairs into feasibility.' No table, plot, or numerical data is provided for the functional relationship between R_s and T_eff for Q1/Q2.

Q3 and Q4 optimization values (T_eff_opt_s: 9.40, 7.50) differ from validation values (T_eff_val_s: 9.32, 7.43). Paper reports only validation values without acknowledging or explaining the optimization-validation discrepancy (coarse vs fine discretization).
Paper body contains internal HTML comments (lines 279-295: Humanizer-zh Self-Check and paper writer self-check) that must be stripped before submission.

Figure numbering jumps from Figure 4 (Section 5.4) to Figure 6 (Section 5.5) with no Figure 5. Code correctly suppressed Figure 5 when Q5 intervals were empty, but the gap in numbering is confusing to readers.
